Electricity and Circuits

Interactive revision on circuit symbols, complete circuits, series circuits, parallel circuits, static electricity and calculations.

🏠 Home Symbols Series Parallel Calculations Static CFU

1. Circuit Symbols and Complete Circuits

LearnA working circuit must be a complete loop. If the switch is open, the circuit is broken and the lamp will not light.

Cell

Supplies energy to the circuit.

Lamp

Transfers energy to light and thermal energy.

Switch

Opens or closes the circuit.

Key sentence: “The circuit works because there is a complete loop from one end of the cell to the other.”

Try itChoose the correct function for each component.

Cell

Lamp

Switch

CheckToggle the switch and decide if the circuit works.

Switch positionclosed
Circuit statuscomplete
Lampon
The switch is closed, so the loop is complete and the lamp lights.

2. Series Circuits

LearnIn a series circuit, components are connected in one loop. The same current flows through every component.

Cell voltage6 V
Lamps in series1 lamp
Run modelcurrent flow
Total resistance3 Ω
Current2.0 A
Brightnessbright
One lamp: lower total resistance, larger current and a brighter lamp.

CheckWhy do lamps become dimmer when another identical lamp is added in series?

3. Parallel Circuits

LearnIn a parallel circuit, each branch has its own complete path. Each branch gets the full cell voltage.

Branch B is open, so lamp 2 is off. Branch A still has a complete path, so lamp 1 stays on.
Cell voltage6 V
Branch Bclosed
Run modelcurrent flow
Voltage per branch6 V
Branch A current2.0 A
Branch B current2.0 A
Total current4.0 A
Total resistance1.5 Ω
Visible statusboth lamps on
Both branches are closed, so both lamps work and each branch gets the full cell voltage.

CheckWhy can one lamp stay on in a parallel circuit when another branch is open?

4. Current, Voltage and Resistance

LearnUse the equation I = V ÷ R.

Current, I

Measured in amps (A).

Voltage, V

Measured in volts (V).

Resistance, R

Measured in ohms (Ω).

Try itChange the values and watch the model update.

Voltage6 V
Resistance3 Ω
Current2.0 A
I = V ÷ R = 6 ÷ 3 = 2.0 A

CheckA lamp has a resistance of 4 Ω and is connected to a 12 V cell. Calculate the current.

5. Static Electricity

LearnRubbing can transfer charge. A charged balloon can attract neutral paper and bend a thin stream of water.

Paper attraction

←
Model answer: The balloon becomes charged after rubbing. The charged balloon attracts the neutral paper, so the paper moves towards it.

Water stream attraction

→
Model answer: The charged balloon attracts the water, so the thin stream bends towards the balloon.

CheckExplain why the water bends towards the balloon.

6. Final CFU: 20 Questions

Secure ItBuild ItStretch It This CFU includes recall, lots of calculations, and written explanation questions.

Secure It: core knowledge

1. Which component supplies energy to a circuit?

Model answer: A cell supplies energy to the circuit.

2. If a switch is open, the circuit is...

Model answer: An open switch breaks the circuit, so current cannot flow.

3. In a series circuit, components are connected in...

Model answer: A series circuit has one loop/path for the current.

4. In a parallel circuit, each branch gets the full...

Model answer: Each branch in parallel receives the full cell voltage.

Build It: calculation practice

5. Calculate the current: V = 6 V, R = 3 Ω.

Model answer: I = V ÷ R = 6 ÷ 3 = 2 A.

6. Calculate the current: V = 12 V, R = 4 Ω.

Model answer: I = V ÷ R = 12 ÷ 4 = 3 A.

7. Calculate the current: V = 9 V, R = 3 Ω.

Model answer: I = V ÷ R = 9 ÷ 3 = 3 A.

8. Calculate the current: V = 10 V, R = 5 Ω.

Model answer: I = V ÷ R = 10 ÷ 5 = 2 A.

9. Two 3 Ω lamps are connected in series. What is the total resistance?

Model answer: Rtotal = 3 Ω + 3 Ω = 6 Ω.

10. Two 6 Ω lamps are connected in series. What is the total resistance?

Model answer: Rtotal = 6 Ω + 6 Ω = 12 Ω.

11. Two 6 Ω lamps are connected in series to a 12 V cell. Calculate the current.

Model answer: Total resistance = 12 Ω. I = V ÷ R = 12 ÷ 12 = 1 A.

12. A 12 V cell is connected to a 4 Ω lamp and an 8 Ω lamp in series. Calculate the current.

Model answer: Total resistance = 4 Ω + 8 Ω = 12 Ω. I = 12 ÷ 12 = 1 A.

13. Two 3 Ω branches are connected in parallel to a 6 V cell. What is the current in each branch?

Model answer: Each branch gets 6 V. I = 6 ÷ 3 = 2 A in each branch.

14. If each branch current is 2 A and there are two branches, what is the total current?

Model answer: Total current = 2 A + 2 A = 4 A.

15. In a parallel circuit, branch A current is 1 A and branch B current is 3 A. What is the total current?

Model answer: Total current = 1 A + 3 A = 4 A.

16. A lamp has a current of 2 A and a resistance of 3 Ω. Calculate the voltage. Use V = I × R.

Model answer: V = I × R = 2 × 3 = 6 V.

Stretch It: explain and compare

17. Explain why adding a second lamp in series makes both lamps dimmer.

Model answer: Adding a lamp in series increases total resistance. With the same voltage, current decreases, so both lamps become dimmer.

18. Explain why a lamp can stay on in one branch of a parallel circuit even if the other branch is open.

Model answer: In a parallel circuit, each branch has its own path. If one branch is open, the other branch can still be complete, so the lamp stays on.

19. Compare the voltage in series and parallel circuits.

Model answer: In series, the cell voltage is shared between components. In parallel, each branch receives the full cell voltage.

20. Explain why a charged balloon can bend a thin stream of water.

Model answer: The balloon becomes charged. It attracts the water, so the thin stream bends towards the balloon.
Score: 0/20
